How to Manage Money in China as a Foreigner
A comprehensive guide to handling money in China - from currency exchange to mobile payments, with embedded tools for everyday calculations.

import CurrencyConverter from '../../components/tools/CurrencyConverter.astro';

# How to Manage Money in China as a Foreigner

China operates almost entirely on digital payments. Cash is rarely accepted in cities, making it essential to understand how to manage your money digitally. This guide covers everything you need to know.

## The Two Main Payment Apps

### Alipay (支付宝)
The most popular payment platform in China. Read our [full Alipay guide](/en/guides/how-to-use-alipay-as-foreigner) for setup instructions.

### WeChat Pay (微信支付)
Integrated with WeChat, China's dominant messaging app. Both apps work similarly for foreigners now.

## Currency Exchange

When you first arrive, you'll need Chinese Yuan (CNY). Here are your options:

### Airport Exchanges
Convenient but typically offer poor rates. Only exchange enough to get you started.

### Bank Exchanges
Better rates than airports. Most major banks (ICBC, Bank of China, HSBC) offer exchange services. Bring your passport.

### ATM Withdrawals
ATMs are everywhere. Your home bank will likely charge foreign transaction fees, so check before traveling.

## Live Currency Conversion

Use this calculator to check current exchange rates before your trip:

<CurrencyConverter />

## Cost Estimates (CNY)

| Item | Approximate Cost |
|------|------------------|
| Metro ride | ¥3-9 |
| Coffee at Starbucks | ¥30-40 |
| Budget meal | ¥20-40 |
| Mid-range restaurant | ¥80-200 |
| Taxi (per km) | ¥2-3 |
| Movie ticket | ¥35-60 |

## Tips for Saving Money

1. **Use Alipay/WeChat Pay** — Many places give discounts for digital payments
2. **Check your bank's foreign transaction fees** — Consider a travel card like Wise or Revolut
3. **Use metro over taxis** — Saves ¥50-100 per trip
4. **Shop at local markets** — Prices are negotiable and much lower than tourist areas

## Important Notes

> ⚠️ **Credit cards are rarely accepted** outside major hotels and international chains. Always carry some cash and have your payment apps set up before arriving.

> 💡 **WeChat Pay and Alipay are now available** to foreign visitors with international bank cards, though setup can be tricky. See our detailed guides for step-by-step instructions.
外国人使用支付宝完全指南
完整指南：如何用外国手机号和国际银行卡注册并使用支付宝。

# 外国人使用支付宝完全指南

支付宝是中国最主要的支付平台。本指南帮助外国人在中国顺利使用支付宝。

## 注册支付宝

### 第一步：下载应用
从应用商店下载支付宝应用，支持英文界面。

### 第二步：用外国手机号注册
支持使用国际手机号（+1、+44 等）注册，确保能够接收短信验证码。

### 第三步：绑定国际银行卡
支付宝现已支持维萨（Visa）、万事达（Mastercard）、美国运通（American Express）等国际卡片。在支付设置中寻找"国际银行卡"选项。

## 如何付款

1. 打开支付宝，点击"付钱"
2. 扫描商家的二维码
3. 确认金额
4. 输入密码或使用面容 ID

## 注意事项

- 部分商家只支持微信支付
- 国际银行卡有每日交易限额
- 某些功能需要中国身份证验证

## 小贴士

- 始终保留备用支付方式（现金）
- 下载离线地图以防支付应用故障
- 学会说"支付宝"——大多数商家都能理解

How to Use Alipay as a Foreigner in China
A complete guide to setting up and using Alipay with a foreign phone number and international bank card.

# How to Use Alipay as a Foreigner in China

Alipay is the dominant payment platform in China. Here's everything you need to know to use it as a foreigner.

## Setting Up Alipay

### Step 1: Download the App
Download Alipay from your app store. The app supports English interface.

### Step 2: Register with Foreign Phone Number
You can register using your international phone number (+1, +44, etc.). Make sure you have access to receive SMS verification codes.

### Step 3: Add Your International Bank Card
Alipay now supports international cards from major networks (Visa, Mastercard, American Express). Look for the "International cards" option in the payment settings.

## Making Payments

1. Open Alipay and tap the "Pay" button
2. Scan the merchant's QR code
3. Confirm the amount
4. Enter your password or use Face ID

## Limitations

- Some merchants only accept WeChat Pay
- Daily transaction limits apply to international cards
- Some features require Chinese ID verification

## Tips

- Always keep a backup payment method (cash)
- Download offline maps in case payment apps fail
- Learn to say "Alipay" (支付宝) — most merchants will understand

How to Navigate Shanghai Metro as a Foreigner
A visual guide to using Shanghai's world-class metro system, including tips for buying tickets, reading signs, and transferring lines.

import ResponsiveImage from '../../components/content/ResponsiveImage.astro';
import ResponsiveVideo from '../../components/content/ResponsiveVideo.astro';

# How to Navigate Shanghai Metro as a Foreigner

Shanghai's metro system is one of the longest and most efficient in the world. With 20+ lines and 500+ stations, it can seem intimidating at first—but this guide will have you navigating like a local in no time.

## Getting a Metro Card

There are two main options for paying your metro fare:

### Option 1: Single Journey Ticket
Purchase at any station's ticket machine. The machines have English interface options.

1. Select your destination station on the touchscreen
2. Insert cash or scan QR code for payment
3. Collect your ticket from the slot below

### Option 2: Shanghai Public Transportation Card (交通卡)
This rechargeable card works on metro, buses, and even some taxis. Get one at any station service counter with a refundable ¥20 deposit.

<ResponsiveImage
  src="https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1558618666-fcd25c85cd64?w=800"
  alt="Shanghai Metro station entrance"
  caption="Shanghai Metro stations are well-marked with both Chinese and English signage"
/>

## Reading Signs and Navigation Apps

### Key Chinese Phrases

| Chinese | Pinyin | English |
|---------|--------|---------|
| 地铁 | Dìtiě | Metro |
| 入口 | Rùkǒu | Entrance |
| 出口 | Chūkǒu | Exit |
| 换乘 | Huàanchéng | Transfer |

### Recommended Apps

1. **Metro Man** — Offline metro maps, works without internet
2. **Apple Maps / Google Maps** — Both work well for metro navigation in Shanghai
3. **SH metro** — Official app with real-time arrival info

<ResponsiveImage
  src="https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1596472306357-be1a5ddadb36?w=800"
  alt="Metro signage in Shanghai"
  caption="All major stations have bilingual signage in Chinese and English"
/>

## Watch This: How to Buy a Ticket

<ResponsiveVideo
  youtube="dQw4w9WgXcQ"
  caption="Step-by-step guide to purchasing metro tickets at Shanghai stations"
/>

## Line-Specific Tips

### Line 2 (East-West Line)
- Connects Pudong Airport to Hongqiao Airport
- Most crowded during rush hour (8-9am, 6-7pm)
- Key stations: People's Square, Lujiazui (financial district)

### Line 10 (Northwest Line)
- Serves many expat neighborhoods
- Connects to Nanjing Road East for shopping

## Pro Tips

> 💡 **Pro tip:** During rush hour, the last carriage is often less crowded. If you're carrying luggage, avoid lines 2 and 11 during peak hours.

- Metro cards can be topped up at convenience stores (FamilyMart, Lawson, 7-11)
- Keep your card—you'll need it to exit through the turnstiles
- Trains arrive every 2-3 minutes during peak hours
- All stations have air conditioning in summer

<ResponsiveImage
  src="https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1580654712603-eb43273aff33?w=800"
  alt="Inside a Shanghai Metro train"
  caption="Shanghai Metro trains are clean, air-conditioned, and run with impressive frequency"
/>

## Emergency and Assistance

If you need help:
- Look for station staff in blue vests
- Each platform has an emergency intercom
- Police stations are available at major transfer hubs
Convert between major world currencies with live exchange rates.

This currency converter helps you convert between major world currencies using live exchange rates from the Frankfurter API.

## Supported Currencies

- **USD** — US Dollar
- **EUR** — Euro
- **CNY** — Chinese Yuan
- **GBP** — British Pound
- **JPY** — Japanese Yen
- **AUD** — Australian Dollar
- **CAD** — Canadian Dollar

## How to Use

Enter an amount in any currency field, and the tool will automatically calculate conversions for all other currencies in real-time.

## Data Source

Exchange rates are provided by [Frankfurter API](https://www.frankfurter.app/), updated daily from the European Central Bank.

## Disclaimer

These rates are for informational purposes only. For exact conversions, please check with your bank or a financial institution.
